images.caption: Figure 1:Three-distribution comparison across speaker encoders on the held-out 1043-pair corpus. For each encoder, the green box is the within-script (same voice, same lang) cosine distribution; blue is cross-script (same voice, different lang); red is across-speaker (noise floor). LASE r1 collapses the within-vs-cross gap (blue and green almost touch) while pushing the noise floor far below (large blue-vs-red margin).

Figure 2:LASE r1 training: speaker contrastive loss drops while the language adversarial loss is held nearln⁡4\ln 4(the 4-class uniform). The encoder learns voice identity but does not learn language, by design.
